fault_hypothesis: On line 75, the comparison uses strict `>` instead of `>=`, so when `used + request` exactly equals `effective_cap`, the function incorrectly returns `True` instead of `False`.

constraints.intent: The strict > check should be >= so that equality also triggers the False branch; this expression is False when used+request==effective_cap.

spec.expr: not (used__1 + request == effective_cap__1) or (used__1 + request >= effective_cap__1)

constraints.intent: When used+request exactly equals effective_cap, the >= condition must hold to correctly deny the request.
In the else branch (returning True), used+request must be strictly less than effective_cap, not merely not greater.
The function should return True only when used+request is strictly less than effective_cap.
